commands.repair_reason: Human review should decide the Canvas plugin/default and canvas.eval security boundary before any repair is attempted; missing real macOS proof also blocks merge.; Needs attention: The diff broadens a security-sensitive node command allowlist and leaves the intended canvas.eval opt-in unenforced in the active plugin path.; Review finding: [P1] Keep Canvas command defaults plugin-owned — src/gateway/node-command-poli... (sha=51f51fec2fc3b43d262d0e613dc4cedfaa017656)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds macOS Canvas command defaults and a `canvas.eval` dangerous-command classification in the gateway node-command policy, plus unit coverage for those allowlist decisions.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +62. Total +75 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. the linked report has concrete live steps from `v2026.5.22`, but current main and the latest release source already register Canvas defaults through the enabled-by-default Canvas plugin. I cannot confirm the failing current-main path without the reporter's plugin config or a real macOS node run.
commands.review_followup: Human review should decide the Canvas plugin/default and `canvas.eval` security boundary before any repair is attempted; missing real macOS proof also blocks merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): allow macOS canvas node commands
target.body:
## Summary
- add a macOS platform default allowlist for canvas UI/control node commands
- keep canvas.eval opt-in as a dangerous command
- cover declared macOS canvas commands from native platform labels

## Test
- COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts

Closes #86707
target.author: stevenepalmer
